chunk_summary: This section provides case-based guidance on stunting workup and counseling, covering under-five, red-flag, and adolescent scenarios with tailored interventions and follow-up strategies.
learning_objectives: Identify and classify stunting in children using HAZ scores and clinical indicators.
Apply determinant-based interventions, including dietary, WASH, and micronutrient strategies.
Recognize red flags for testing and manage coexisting conditions like malabsorption or anemia.
Counsel families on growth monitoring, dietary improvements, and follow-up thresholds.
key_facts: HAZ -2.6 indicates stunting without wasting in a 20-month-old child.
Recurrent diarrhea requires ORS, zinc, and deworming/vitamin A status checks.
Coeliac serology (tTG-IgA and total IgA) is essential for suspected malabsorption.
Adolescents with delayed menarche and low growth velocity need endocrine evaluation if no progress in 6 months.
WASH interventions include safe water storage, soap at critical points, and clean play spaces.
reference_quote: Stunting can coexist with a treatable intestinal disorder; initiate determinant actions while awaiting results. (Source: WHO, 2023)
document_key_topics: Adolescent growth and double burden of malnutrition
